title: Floral Light Japanese Snow Peas Stir-Fry
description: A fragrant, airy one-pot stir-fry featuring crisp snow peas, baby spinach, and sweet shiitake mushrooms, finished with a citrusy soy‑mirin glaze and a sprinkle of toasted sesame and edible blossoms.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large non‑stick skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms and sauté for 2 minutes until they release their aroma.
Introduce the snow peas and shredded carrots, stir‑frying for 3 minutes until they start to soften but remain crisp.
Add the baby spinach and continue to stir until the greens just wilt.
Whisk together soy sauce, mirin, rice vinegar, sugar, and white pepper in a small bowl, then pour it over the vegetables, stirring to coat evenly.
Cook for another 2 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly and the snow peas to become tender but still bright green.
Remove from heat and transfer the stir‑fry to a serving dish.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds and sliced scallion over the top for a fresh finish.
Serve immediately, optionally garnishing with a few edible flower petals for added floral flair.
